summary: A symmetrical, rectangular multi-storey mansion with a central long spine and two wider wings at the far end. Walls are light-colored with horizontal banding and many narrow horizontal windows; the roof is steep and made of red brick stair blocks forming a stepped gable along the central spine. A low white block fence outlines the plot and a short entrance path leads to a small front portico.

shape.overall_form: long central corridor (spine) with two larger blocks (wings) at one end; front entrance on the opposite end; building sits inside a rectangular fenced plot
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along the central spine (mirror left/right)

elements: long central roofed spine connecting front entrance to rear twin wings
two main rectangular wings with stepped gable roof on rear end
horizontal narrow windows in continuous bands across floors
white low block decorative fence around perimeter and short entrance walkway
raised base course / belt courses of white trim between floors and at corners
rebuild_hints: Construct a long central corridor (spine) approximately centered in plot; align a stepped brick gable roof along it ending in a taller central chimney/ridge at the wing junction.

Place two wider rectangular wings at the rear of the spine, mirrored left/right; each wing is three storeys tall with continuous 1-block-high horizontal window strips on each floor separated by white trim bands.

Use light sandstone (or smooth sandstone) for main wall infill, quartz (or white blocks) for vertical corner posts, horizontal belt courses and low perimeter fence blocks; use brick stairs and slabs for the sloped stepped roof.
Outline the building footprint with single-height white blocks spaced as seen to form a decorative low fence; create a short front path and small square portico aligned with the central spine.
uncertainties: Exact block IDs: sandstone vs smooth_sandstone vs sand (I labeled primary wall as 'sandstone' with high confidence but texture variant is uncertain).
Interior layout and floor heights are not visible; floors_estimate is based on three visible horizontal window bands but internal divisions may differ.
Precise footprint dimensions (width/depth) are estimated from camera perspectives; actual block counts may vary by a few blocks.
The fence blocks appear white (likely quartz) but could be another white block (e.g., wool, concrete); chosen as quartz with medium confidence.
